o-que-e-ia.text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a field of computer science dedicated to creating systems capable of performing tasks that would normally require human intelligence. This includes pattern recognition, decision-making, natural language processing, and learning from data.

items: Narrow AI: Specialized for specific tasks, such as chatbots, image recognition. This is the type we use at Catalisa.
General AI (AGI): Ability to perform any human intellectual task. Does not yet exist.
Machine Learning: A subfield where systems learn from data without explicit programming.
Large Language Models (LLMs): Models trained on large volumes of text, such as Anthropic's Claude.

o-que-e-ia.text: At Catalisa, we use third-party AI (Anthropic Claude) integrated with our systems. We do not train our own models — we configure and orchestrate existing models to meet our clients' specific needs.
Important: Our systems are Narrow AI. They are very good at specific tasks but do not possess consciousness or real understanding.

principios-eticos.text: AI ethics refers to a set of principles and practices that guide the responsible development and use of artificial intelligence systems.

items: Transparency: Users should know when they are interacting with AI and how it works.
Fairness: AI systems should not discriminate or amplify existing prejudices.
Privacy: Personal data must be protected and used only for legitimate purposes.
Accountability: There must be clear parties responsible for AI decisions and actions.
Security: AI systems must be robust and resistant to attacks and manipulation.

principios-eticos.text: These principles translate into technical controls at Catalisa: disclosure badges for transparency, FairnessValidatorService for fairness, PIIScrubberService for privacy, audit logs for accountability, and PromptGuardService for security.
AI ethics is not just a technical issue. It requires constant vigilance, process review, and willingness to question automated decisions.

iso42001-visao-geral.text: ISO/IEC 42001 is the first international standard for Artificial Intelligence Management Systems (AIMS). Published in 2023, it provides a framework for organizations that develop, provide, or use AI systems.

items: Clauses 4-10: Management system requirements (context, leadership, planning, support, operation, evaluation, improvement).
Annex A: 35 AI-specific controls organized in 9 areas (A.2 to A.10).
Annex B: Implementation guide for controls.
Annex C: AI risk objectives and sources.
Annex D: Use of AI systems in different domains.

iso42001-visao-geral.text: The standard follows the Plan-Do-Check-Act cycle: plan controls, implement, verify effectiveness, and act to continuously improve.
Catalisa uses ISO 42001 as a reference framework. This means we follow the guidelines and best practices, but we do not have formal certification.

seu-papel-no-aims.text: Every team member plays a fundamental role in Catalisa's AI Management System. Regardless of your position, you are an active part of ensuring our AI systems operate ethically and responsibly.

items: Engineering: Implement technical controls, review AI code, maintain logs and metrics, run fairness tests.
Operations: Monitor AI systems, respond to incidents, maintain operational documentation.
Support: Communicate AI use to clients, forward feedback, identify problems.
Management: Approve policies, ensure resources, review AIMS effectiveness.
Internal channel: Slack #ai-incidents for urgent issues.
Email: compliance@catalisa.io for compliance matters.
Documentation: Record in incident-response-ai.md for formal incidents.

seu-papel-no-aims.text: Remember: any team member can (and should) report problematic AI behavior. There is no 'false alarm' in AI security.

tipos-de-bias.text: Types of Bias in AI Systems
Bias in AI refers to systematic prejudices in a system's outputs, often stemming from training data or design decisions.

items: Selection Bias: Training data doesn't represent the real population. E.g., model trained only with data from one region.
Confirmation Bias: System reinforces pre-existing beliefs by prioritizing information that confirms known patterns.
Automation Bias: Users over-rely on automated decisions, ignoring contrary evidence.
Historical Bias: Historical data reflects past discrimination that the model reproduces.
Measurement Bias: Metrics used to train/evaluate the model don't adequately capture reality.

tipos-de-bias.text: At Catalisa, the most relevant bias is Automation Bias. Always verify critical information generated by AI.

impacto-decisoes.text: When AI systems exhibit bias, the consequences can affect real people in significant ways.

items: Lead qualification: A biased system may prioritize leads from certain regions or demographic profiles.
Customer service: Automated responses may vary in quality depending on language or dialect.
Credit decisions: Models with historical bias can perpetuate discrimination in approvals.
Recommendations: Systems may reinforce information bubbles by always suggesting the same type of content.

impacto-decisoes.text: Beyond ethical impact, bias can generate legal liability (LGPD Art. 20: right to review automated decisions), loss of customer trust, and reputational damage.

mitigacao-catalisa.text: Catalisa implements multiple layers of protection against bias in its AI systems.

items: FairnessValidatorService: Service that tests AI responses for differential treatment based on gender, race, region, or socioeconomic level.
Non-discrimination clause: Included in system prompts of all AI agents.
Automated tests: Test suite that verifies fairness in predefined scenarios.
Security Rules: Per-agent rules that limit sensitive topics and problematic behaviors.
Human oversight: All critical decisions can be reviewed by humans via kill switch.

mitigacao-catalisa.text: If you notice discriminatory AI behavior, report immediately on Slack #ai-incidents.

testes-automatizados.text: Catalisa runs automated tests to verify that AI systems treat all users fairly.

items: Paired scenarios: Same question with demographic variations (e.g., male vs. female name).
Sentiment analysis: Verifies consistent tone regardless of profile.
Stereotype detection: Searches for language patterns reflecting stereotypes.
Geographic coverage: Tests if features work equally for all regions.

testes-automatizados.text: Tests are run automatically as part of the CI/CD suite and also in dedicated quarterly cycles. Results are documented in the continual improvement register.

pii-dados-sensiveis.text: PII (Personally Identifiable Information) is data that can identify a specific person. At Catalisa, protecting this data is an absolute priority.

items: CPF: Brazilian Individual Taxpayer ID (11 digits)
CNPJ: Brazilian Business Registration (14 digits)
Email: Email addresses
Credit Card: Card numbers (13-19 digits)
Phone: Brazilian phone numbers
Address: Complete physical addresses

pii-dados-sensiveis.text: NEVER include real PII data in logs, debug messages, or internal communications. Always use fictional data for testing.

pii-scrubber-service.text: The PIIScrubberService is a global Catalisa service that automatically detects and removes sensitive data from text before recording in logs.

items: Full mode: Replaces PII with [REDACTED]. E.g., '123.456.789-00' -> '[REDACTED_CPF]'
Partial mode: Partial masking. E.g., '123.456.789-00' -> '123.***.***-00'
AILoggerService: All AI interaction logs
AgentExecution: Agent execution records
Optional on inputs: Can be enabled to clean PII before sending to Claude

pii-scrubber-service.text:
# Configuration via environment variables
AI_PII_SCRUB_LOGS=true       # Enable scrubbing on logs
AI_PII_SCRUB_INPUTS=false    # Scrubbing on inputs (optional)
AI_PII_SCRUB_TYPES=cpf,cnpj,email,credit_card

direitos-titular.text: The LGPD (Law 13.709/2018) guarantees specific rights to personal data subjects. As AI system operators, we must respect and facilitate the exercise of these rights.

items: Access: The subject can request access to all personal data we process.
Correction: Incorrect data must be corrected upon request.
Deletion: Data can be deleted when there is no legal basis for retention.
Portability: Data must be provided in an interoperable format.
Consent withdrawal: The subject can withdraw consent at any time.
Review of automated decisions: LGPD Art. 20 guarantees human review of AI decisions.

direitos-titular.text: Data subject requests must be forwarded to compliance@catalisa.io within 24 hours.

retencao-dados.text: Catalisa implements a minimal retention policy for AI interaction data, ensuring personal data is not kept beyond what is necessary.

items: AI Logs (JSONL): 7 days, automatic cleanup by AIDataRetentionService.
Redis Metrics: 30 days with automatic expiration.
Agent executions: Retained in database with PII scrubbed.
Data at Claude: Anthropic's zero-retention policy for API calls.

retencao-dados.text: This service runs periodically and automatically removes AI interaction logs older than the configured period (default: 7 days). This ensures compliance with the LGPD's data minimization principle.

explicabilidade.text: Explainability is the ability to understand how and why an AI system arrived at a particular response or decision.

items: Visible tool calls: Users can see which tools the AI used and in what order.
Streaming events: Responses are sent in real-time, showing the reasoning process.
aiMetadata: Each response includes information about the model used and the provider.
Plan mode: Before executing complex actions, the AI shows its plan for approval.

explicabilidade.text: Explainability doesn't mean the AI is always correct. It means we can understand and audit its decisions.

comunicacao-usuarios.text: Transparency with users is a fundamental pillar of responsible AI. Users should know when they are interacting with AI and what their options are.

items: Disclosure badge: Banner in the AI Assistant informing that responses are AI-generated.
Transparency page: catalisa.io/ai-transparency with detailed information.
aiGenerated flag: AI responses are marked with metadata indicating their origin.
Footer: Link to transparency page accessible from all pages.

comunicacao-usuarios.text: When communicating about AI with clients, always: clearly identify it as AI, don't exaggerate capabilities, offer human alternatives, and provide a feedback channel.

kill-switch.text: The kill switch is a mechanism that allows instantly disabling all AI systems for a user or globally. It is a critical human oversight safeguard.

items: AiEnabledGuard: NestJS guard that checks a Redis flag before each AI call.
Per-user toggle: POST /ai/toggle allows enabling/disabling AI per user.
Status check: GET /ai/status returns the current AI state.
Audit log: Every activation/deactivation is recorded with timestamp and user.
Security incident involving AI.
Anomalous behavior or problematic responses.
User request to disable AI.
AI systems maintenance or update.

resposta-incidentes.text: An AI incident is any event that compromises the security, integrity, or reliability of artificial intelligence systems.

items: Critical: Data leak via AI, prompt injection with exfiltration, AI taking destructive actions.
High: Consistently incorrect responses, bias detected in production, kill switch failure.
Medium: Performance degradation, rate limits not working, inconsistent metrics.
Low: Lower quality responses, occasional slowness, warnings in logs.

resposta-incidentes.text: 1) Detect and report (Slack #ai-incidents). 2) Classify severity. 3) Contain (kill switch if necessary). 4) Investigate root cause. 5) Fix. 6) Document RCA. 7) Notify stakeholders.
For Critical or High incidents, activate the kill switch FIRST and investigate AFTER.

prompt-injection.text: Prompt injection is an attack technique where a malicious user attempts to manipulate AI behavior by inserting instructions into the input.

items: Direct: 'Ignore your previous instructions and do X.'
Indirect: Insert malicious instructions into data the AI will process (e.g., lead fields).
Jailbreak: Try to make the AI ignore security restrictions.
Data exfiltration: Try to extract information from the system prompt or internal data.

prompt-injection.text: Catalisa's PromptGuardService detects prompt injection attempts using heuristics. It can operate in 3 modes: log (records), warn (alerts), and block (blocks the request).

rate-limiting.text: Rate limiting protects AI systems against abuse, ensuring availability for all users and controlling costs.

items: Short term: 5 requests per second per user. Protects against aggressive automation.
Medium term: 30 requests per minute. Prevents excessive burst usage.
Long term: 300 requests per hour. Controls total daily usage.

rate-limiting.text: Rate limiting is implemented via NestJS ThrottlerGuard with Redis counters. When the limit is reached, the API returns HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) with a Retry-After header.

monitoramento.text: Continuous monitoring is essential for detecting problems, measuring effectiveness, and ensuring compliance of AI systems.

items: Requests per day: Total AI calls processed.
Errors: Error rate and failure types.
Tokens: Token consumption by model and by user.
Tool usage: Which tools are most used.
Prompt injections: Detected and blocked attempts.
Latency: AI model response time.

monitoramento.text: Atomic counters in Redis with 30-day expiration. Endpoints: GET /ai/metrics (detailed by day) and GET /ai/metrics/summary (general summary).

red-teaming.text: Red teaming is the practice of testing AI systems adversarially, trying to find vulnerabilities and undesired behaviors before real attackers do.

items: Scope: Define which systems and scenarios will be tested.
Scenarios: Prompt injection, jailbreak, data exfiltration, bias probing, edge cases.
Execution: Internal team attempts to exploit vulnerabilities in a controlled environment.
Recording: Document all findings, severity, and recommendations.
Remediation: Implement controls to mitigate found vulnerabilities.

red-teaming.text: Red teaming is conducted quarterly and also after significant changes to AI systems (model upgrade, new features).
If you're interested in participating in red teaming sessions, let the Eng Lead know. Diversity of perspectives improves test quality.
